stemHtml: An aircraft is flying straight and level when it experiences moderate turbulence. The pilot observes a rapid reduction in IAS towards zero, while all other instrument indications are unchanged. What should the pilot do?

options.a: Ensure that the existing aircraft attitude and thrust settings remain unchanged.
options.b: Immediately apply full power to restore IAS.
options.c: Immediately pitch the aircraft down to avoid a stall.
options.d: Switch off anti-icing equipment, which may have malfunctioned.
